title: session_hijacking
description: An attacker on an unsecured public WiFi network captures an employee's session cookie transmitted over an unencrypted connection and uses it to impersonate the user inside a corporate application.

timeline.title: Anomalous Session Activity Detected
timeline.description: The application logs show the same active session token being used from two different IP addresses simultaneously.

timeline.title: Session Cookie Theft Confirmed
timeline.description: Network analysis confirms the session token was transmitted over an unencrypted HTTP connection, where it was captured by the attacker.

timeline.title: Attacker Active in Application
timeline.description: The attacker is accessing HR documents and attempting to change the account password using the hijacked session.

timeline.title: Session Invalidated
timeline.description: All active sessions for the affected user are invalidated. The attacker is immediately ejected from the application.

timeline.title: Damage Assessment Underway
timeline.description: Application audit logs reviewed to determine which records were accessed and what actions were taken during the hijacked session.

timeline.title: Application Security Review Initiated
timeline.description: HTTPS enforced across all application endpoints. Session token lifetime reduced and VPN mandate introduced for remote access.

node-1.title: Duplicate Session Detected
node-1.situation: An employee reports being suddenly logged out of the corporate HR application while using free WiFi at a coffee shop. Application logs show the same session token is now being actively used from a different IP address in another city.
node-1.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately invalidate all active sessions for the affected user account and force re-authentication.

options.label: B
options.text: Ask the user to log back in and monitor the situation before taking action.

options.label: C
options.text: Block only the suspicious IP address and leave the original session active.

node-2.title: Session Hijacking Confirmed
node-2.situation: Analysis confirms the session token was transmitted over an unencrypted HTTP connection at the coffee shop, where it was captured by a network eavesdropper. The attacker used it to authenticate directly, bypassing the login page entirely. The session has been invalidated, but the attacker was active for approximately 8 minutes.
node-2.question: How do you limit the damage from the 8-minute window of access?

options.label: A
options.text: Review all actions taken during the hijacked session, identify every record accessed, and notify owners of any sensitive data that was viewed.

options.label: B
options.text: Simply block the attacker's IP address and consider the session closed.

node-3.title: Sensitive Data Accessed and Password Change Attempted
node-3.situation: Audit logs show the hijacked session was used to view 12 confidential HR records and initiate a password change request (which was sent to the employee's email before being acted on). The password was not successfully changed.
node-3.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Cancel the pending password change, notify the data owners of the 12 accessed HR records, and conduct a data breach assessment.

options.label: B
options.text: Reset the application password and take no further action since the session is now closed.

node-4.title: Preventing Future Session Hijacking
node-4.situation: The data breach assessment is underway. Leadership wants to know how to prevent session hijacking across all corporate applications.
node-4.question: What controls do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Enforce HTTPS across all applications with HSTS, implement session binding to device fingerprint and IP, reduce session token lifetime, and mandate VPN use for remote access to sensitive systems.

options.label: B
options.text: Only enforce HTTPS on the single affected application.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The hijacked session was invalidated and the pending password change cancelled. Affected data owners have been notified and a breach assessment is complete. HTTPS with HSTS is now enforced across all corporate applications, session lifetimes are reduced, and a VPN mandate is in place for remote access to sensitive systems.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
